cards.front: ![[mega-lab-topology.svg]]
cards.back: Topology summary: R-ISP ↔ R1 (200.1.1.0/30, OSPF area 0). R1 g0/1 trunk → SW1 with ROAS sub-interfaces .10/.20/.99. SW1 ↔ SW2 via LACP Po1. End hosts in VLANs 10/20/99. R1 hosts DHCP for VLAN 10 + NAT/PAT outbound + OSPF + SSH-only mgmt.

cards.front: **STEP 1** — Initial baseline on R1 (hostname, banner, console + enable secret):
cards.back:
```
hostname R1
no ip domain-lookup
ip domain-name lab.local
enable secret StrongPass123!
banner motd # Authorized access only #

cards.front: **STEP 2** — SSH-only VTY on R1:
cards.back:
```
username admin privilege 15 secret AdminPass1!
crypto key generate rsa modulus 2048
ip ssh version 2

cards.front: **STEP 3** — Configure R1 outside interface (to R-ISP):
cards.back:
```
interface g0/0
 description WAN to R-ISP
 ip address 200.1.1.2 255.255.255.252
 ip nat outside
 no shutdown
```

cards.front: **STEP 4** — Configure R1 trunk port (to SW1, ROAS):
cards.back:
```
interface g0/1
 description Trunk to SW1
 no shutdown                                ! sub-interfaces inherit no-shut
```

cards.front: **STEP 5** — Create sub-interfaces for VLAN 10/20/99 on R1 (ROAS):
cards.back:
```
interface g0/1.10
 description VLAN 10 Data
 encapsulation dot1q 10
 ip address 10.1.10.1 255.255.255.0
 ip nat inside

cards.front: **STEP 6** — Configure OSPF on R1 (single area 0, all directly connected nets):
cards.back:
```
router ospf 1
 router-id 1.1.1.1
 passive-interface default
 no passive-interface g0/0
 network 200.1.1.0 0.0.0.3 area 0
 network 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
 network 10.1.20.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
 network 10.1.99.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
```

cards.front: **STEP 7** — Configure DHCP pool on R1 for VLAN 10 hosts:
cards.back:
```
! Exclude gateway + reserved
ip dhcp excluded-address 10.1.10.1 10.1.10.10

cards.front: **STEP 8** — Configure NAT/PAT (overload) on R1:
cards.back:
```
! ACL matching inside networks to translate
access-list 10 permit 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255
access-list 10 permit 10.1.20.0 0.0.0.255

cards.front: **STEP 9** — Configure default route on R1 toward ISP:
**STEP 10** — Apply extended ACL on R1 to block external SSH to VLAN 99:
cards.back:
```
ip access-list extended WAN-IN
 deny tcp any 10.1.99.0 0.0.0.255 eq 22
 permit ip any any

cards.front: **STEP 11** — Initial baseline on SW1 (same as R1: hostname, secrets, SSH):
cards.back:
```
hostname SW1
ip domain-name lab.local
enable secret StrongPass123!
username admin secret AdminPass1!
crypto key generate rsa modulus 2048
ip ssh version 2

cards.front: **STEP 12** — Create VLANs on SW1 + SW2:
cards.back:
```
vlan 10
 name DATA
vlan 20
 name SERVERS
vlan 99
 name MGMT
```

cards.front: **STEP 13** — Configure management SVI on SW1 (VLAN 99):
cards.back:
```
interface vlan 99
 description Mgmt SVI
 ip address 10.1.99.11 255.255.255.0
 no shutdown

cards.front: **STEP 14** — Configure access ports on SW1 (PC-A in VLAN 10):
cards.back:
```
interface g0/1
 description PC-A
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 10
 spanning-tree portfast
 spanning-tree bpduguard enable
```

cards.front: **STEP 15** — Configure server-VLAN access port on SW1:
cards.back:
```
interface g0/13
 description Server
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 20
 spanning-tree portfast
 spanning-tree bpduguard enable
```

cards.front: **STEP 16** — Configure port security on PC access ports:
cards.back:
```
interface range g0/1 - 12
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 2
 switchport port-security mac-address sticky
 switchport port-security violation restrict
```

cards.front: **STEP 17** — Configure trunk from SW1 to R1:
cards.back:
```
interface g0/24
 description Trunk to R1
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport mode trunk
 switchport trunk native vlan 99
 switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,99
 switchport nonegotiate
```

cards.front: **STEP 18** — Configure LACP EtherChannel from SW1 to SW2 (g0/23 + g0/22):
cards.back:
```
interface range g0/22 - 23
 channel-protocol lacp
 channel-group 1 mode active
 ! interface Port-channel 1 auto-created
interface Port-channel 1
 switchport mode trunk
 switchport trunk native vlan 99
 switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,99
```
Mirror config on SW2 with mode `active` (or `passive`).

cards.front: **STEP 19** — Set SW1 as STP root for VLAN 10 + 20:
cards.back:
```
spanning-tree mode rapid-pvst                ! default on most modern switches
spanning-tree vlan 10,20 root primary
spanning-tree vlan 10,20 root secondary       ! actually set SECONDARY on SW2
```

cards.front: **STEP 20** — Configure NTP client on R1 (using R-ISP as NTP server):
cards.back:
```
ntp server 200.1.1.1
clock timezone CST -6
```

cards.front: **STEP 21** — Enable syslog to a remote collector:
cards.back:
```
logging host 10.1.20.50
logging trap informational
service timestamps log datetime msec
service sequence-numbers
```

cards.front: **STEP 22** — Configure SNMP read-only community + location:
cards.back:
```
snmp-server community LabRO RO
snmp-server location "Algonquin Lab"
snmp-server contact admin@lab.local
```

cards.front: **STEP 23** — VERIFY: R1 interfaces are up + IPs correct:
cards.back:
```
show ip interface brief
show interfaces description
```
All configured interfaces should show `up/up` with the right IP.

cards.front: **STEP 24** — VERIFY: OSPF neighbor formed between R1 and R-ISP:
cards.back:
```
show ip ospf neighbor
```
Should show R-ISP at state **FULL**. If stuck at Init or 2-Way → check Hello timers, area ID, subnet, MTU.

cards.front: **STEP 25** — VERIFY: routes learned via OSPF on R1:
cards.back:
```
show ip route ospf
show ip route 0.0.0.0
```
Should see ISP routes with `O` code + your default route with `S*` or `O*E2`.

cards.front: **STEP 26** — VERIFY: DHCP pool is leasing addresses:
cards.back:
```
show ip dhcp pool
show ip dhcp binding
show ip dhcp conflict
```
Bindings list each client MAC + assigned IP + lease expiry.

cards.front: **STEP 27** — VERIFY: NAT translations are happening:
cards.back:
```
show ip nat translations
show ip nat statistics
clear ip nat translation *                  ! reset to see fresh ones
```
After a host pings out, you should see entries with inside-local + inside-global.

cards.front: **STEP 28** — VERIFY: trunk is operational + native VLAN matches:
cards.back:
```
show interfaces trunk
show interfaces g0/24 switchport
```
"Trunking Native Mode VLAN" should be 99 on both sides.

cards.front: **STEP 29** — VERIFY: EtherChannel bundled correctly:
cards.back:
```
show etherchannel summary
```
Look for `P` flag (in port-channel) on both member ports. `Po1(SU)` = layer-2, in use.

cards.front: **STEP 30** — VERIFY: STP root is SW1 for VLANs 10/20:
cards.back:
```
show spanning-tree root
show spanning-tree vlan 10
```
"This bridge is the root" should appear on SW1's output.

cards.front: **STEP 31** — VERIFY: VLAN assignments on SW1:
cards.back:
```
show vlan brief
show interfaces switchport
```
Each access port should show its admin + operational VLAN.

cards.front: **STEP 32** — VERIFY: port security is enforcing:
cards.back:
```
show port-security
show port-security interface g0/1
show port-security address
```
Check: configured max, current count, sticky MACs learned, violation counts.

cards.front: **STEP 33** — VERIFY: SSH-only management works:
cards.back:
```
show ip ssh
show ssh
! From another device:
ssh -l admin 10.1.99.11
```
Telnet should be refused; SSH should prompt for credentials.

cards.front: **STEP 34** — VERIFY: end-to-end host reachability + ACL behavior:
cards.back:
```
! From PC-A (VLAN 10):
ping 8.8.8.8                                ! should succeed (NAT works)
ping 10.1.20.50                             ! should succeed (inter-VLAN routing works)

cards.front: **STEP 35** — Save running config to startup on all devices (don't lose work on reload):
cards.back:
```
copy running-config startup-config
! or
wr
```
On each device. Always do this LAST after verifying everything works.

cards.front: **STEP 36** — Backup config to TFTP server (good habit before changes):
cards.back:
```
copy running-config tftp:
! prompts for host + filename
copy running-config flash:R1-2026-05-25.cfg ! local backup
```
